meta.title: AgriSage Crop Disease & Treatment Reference Knowledge Base (Korea-Registration Basis, English)
meta.purpose: Ground-truth reference data for the RAG pipeline. The LLM must generate explanations and recommendations grounded only in this file — it must not invent symptoms, product names, or safety intervals.
meta.source_alignment: Class taxonomy matches the PlantVillage / New Plant Diseases Dataset (Kaggle) 38-class structure (14 crops).
meta.regulatory_framework: Based on South Korea's Pesticide Control Act and the Positive List System (PLS), fully enforced since Jan 1, 2019. Under PLS, only pesticides formally registered for a specific crop may be used on that crop — even a pesticide registered for a similar disease on a different crop cannot legally be used unless it is separately registered for that exact crop.
meta.authoritative_source: Rural Development Administration (RDA) Pesticide Safety Information System (PSIS), https://psis.rda.go.kr — the exact registered brand names and safety-use standards (days before harvest, maximum application count) per crop-disease pair must always be confirmed here before deployment.
meta.disclaimer: The active-ingredient categories listed in this file (e.g., mancozeb, chlorothalonil) represent commonly registered ingredient classes typically used against each pathogen type in Korea, provided for reference only. Before recommending any product to a user for a specific crop, the exact registered brand name and safety-use standard (application timing, pre-harvest interval, maximum spray count) must be confirmed via PSIS for that exact crop-disease combination. Recommending an unregistered combination may constitute a PLS violation.

classes.symptoms: Olive-to-black irregular spots on leaves and fruit; leaves may curl or drop early in severe cases. Fruit surface develops hard, corky lesions.
classes.causal_conditions: Spreads during cool, wet weather from bloom through shoot growth (roughly April–June). Worsens in densely planted, poorly ventilated orchards.

treatment.application_method: Foliar spray, covering the full canopy evenly
treatment.application_timing: Preventive sprays from just before bloom through shoot growth every 7–10 days; prioritize spraying ahead of forecast rain
treatment.resistance_management: Avoid consecutive sprays of the same class (e.g., triazoles); rotate with a different-class product
treatment.cultural_control: Remove and destroy fallen leaves and infected twigs, prune for better airflow, plant resistant varieties
treatment.phi_note: Typically restricted 14–21 days before harvest (varies by product; confirm via PSIS)
treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.korea_registration_note: Search PSIS (https://psis.rda.go.kr) by 'crop + disease' to confirm the actual registered brand name and exact safety-use standard before use.

classes.symptoms: Brown leaf spots with purple margins ('frog-eye' pattern); fruit develops concentric black rot; cankers form on branches.
classes.causal_conditions: Hot, humid conditions; infection commonly enters through wounds (pruning cuts, hail damage).

treatment.application_method: Foliar spray; apply protective wound dressing to pruning cuts
treatment.application_timing: Preventive sprays from bloom through fruit development; protect wounds immediately after pruning
treatment.resistance_management: Avoid repeated use of the same mode of action
treatment.cultural_control: Remove and destroy infected branches and fruit, minimize wounding, apply wound sealant right after pruning
treatment.phi_note: Typically restricted around 14 days before harvest (varies by product; confirm via PSIS)
treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.korea_registration_note: Search PSIS (https://psis.rda.go.kr) by 'crop + disease' to confirm the actual registered brand name and exact safety-use standard before use.

classes.pathogen: Gymnosporangium juniperi-virginianae (fungus, alternates between juniper and apple hosts)
classes.symptoms: Yellow-to-orange spots on the upper leaf surface; tube-like spore structures on the underside. Severe cases cause early leaf drop.
classes.causal_conditions: Higher incidence when juniper/cedar trees (the alternate host) are nearby. Spores spread in humid spring conditions.

treatment.application_timing: Preventive sprays around bloom
treatment.resistance_management: Avoid repeated triazole use; rotate with a protectant
treatment.cultural_control: Remove nearby juniper/cedar trees or maintain sufficient distance; plant resistant varieties
treatment.phi_note: Typically restricted 14–30 days before harvest (varies by product; confirm via PSIS)
treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.symptoms: White, powdery fungal growth on leaves and young shoots; severe cases cause leaf curling and reduced growth.
classes.causal_conditions: Favored by warm, dry days with large day-night humidity swings. Worsens with dense planting and poor airflow.

treatment.application_timing: Preventive during shoot growth; switch to curative treatment immediately upon symptom detection
treatment.resistance_management: Avoid repeated use of the same class
treatment.cultural_control: Prune to improve airflow, avoid excess nitrogen fertilization
treatment.phi_note: Many products allow use up to 0–7 days before harvest, but this varies by active ingredient (confirm via PSIS)
treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.symptoms: Rectangular gray-brown lesions between leaf veins; severe cases cause whole-leaf death.
classes.causal_conditions: Hot, humid conditions; pathogen persists in no-till or continuously cropped fields.

treatment.application_timing: Preventive around tasseling/silking
treatment.resistance_management: Avoid repeated standalone strobilurin use; rotate with triazoles
treatment.cultural_control: Crop rotation, residue removal via tillage, resistant varieties
treatment.phi_note: Typically restricted 14–30 days before harvest (varies by product; confirm via PSIS)
treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.symptoms: Numerous reddish-brown pustules on both leaf surfaces; severe cases cause yellowing.
classes.causal_conditions: Rapid spore spread in cool, humid weather.

treatment.application_timing: Apply immediately upon early symptom detection
treatment.cultural_control: Resistant varieties, early planting to avoid the infection window
treatment.phi_note: Typically restricted 14–21 days before harvest (varies by product; confirm via PSIS)
treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.causal_conditions: Rapid spread in cool (18–27°C), dewy conditions.

treatment.application_timing: Preventive from early symptoms through silking
treatment.cultural_control: Crop rotation, resistant varieties, residue removal
treatment.phi_note: Typically restricted 14–30 days before harvest (varies by product; confirm via PSIS)
treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.symptoms: Brown circular leaf spots with black margins; fruit browns and mummifies black.
classes.causal_conditions: Rapid spread in hot, humid conditions during bloom through fruit set.

treatment.application_method: Foliar spray, covering fruit clusters evenly
treatment.application_timing: Every 7–10 days from just before bloom through veraison
treatment.cultural_control: Remove infected fruit and leaves; prune to improve canopy airflow
treatment.phi_note: Typically around 14–30 days before harvest (varies widely by product; confirm via PSIS)
treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.symptoms: Tiger-stripe pattern of yellowing/necrosis on leaves; small black spots on fruit; chronic infection can kill the vine.
classes.causal_conditions: Enters through pruning wounds; common in older vines. Chemical treatment options are very limited, making prevention essential.

treatment.prevention_active_ingredients: Wound-protectant dressing applied at pruning cuts
treatment.treatment_active_ingredients: Few effective chemical treatments exist — surgical removal of infected wood is the practical response
treatment.application_method: Apply wound dressing immediately after pruning
treatment.application_timing: Immediately after pruning
treatment.cultural_control: Surgically remove infected wood, remove and destroy infected vines early, use certified disease-free planting stock
treatment.phi_note: Not applicable (managed primarily through non-chemical means)
treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.causal_conditions: Hot, humid conditions; higher incidence in densely planted vineyards.

treatment.application_timing: Preventive around the rainy season
treatment.cultural_control: Prune to improve airflow, remove infected leaves
treatment.phi_note: Typically restricted 14–30 days before harvest (varies by product; confirm via PSIS)

classes.pathogen: Candidatus Liberibacter spp. (bacterium, transmitted by the Asian citrus psyllid)
classes.symptoms: Asymmetric, blotchy yellow mottling on leaves; small, lopsided, bitter-tasting fruit.
classes.causal_conditions: Spreads when psyllid vector populations are high. No direct cure exists.

treatment.prevention_active_ingredients: No direct treatment exists — insecticide-based vector (psyllid) control is the key management tool
treatment.treatment_active_ingredients: Neonicotinoid-class and other insecticides targeting the psyllid vector
treatment.application_method: Foliar spray guided by vector scouting
treatment.application_timing: Intensive control during new-shoot flush periods when psyllids are active
treatment.spray_interval: Adjust based on observed vector density
treatment.resistance_management: Rotate insecticide classes to manage vector resistance
treatment.cultural_control: Remove and destroy infected trees early, use certified disease-free nursery stock, install exclusion netting
treatment.phi_note: Safety-use intervals for vector-control insecticides vary by active ingredient (confirm via PSIS)
treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.korea_registration_note: Search PSIS (https://psis.rda.go.kr) by 'crop + disease' to confirm the actual registered brand name and exact safety-use standard before use.
classes.severity_level: very high (can be lethal to the tree)

classes.symptoms: Small brown leaf spots that develop into shot-holes; sunken lesions on fruit surface.
classes.causal_conditions: Hot, humid conditions; spread by rain splash. Worse in areas lacking windbreaks.

treatment.treatment_active_ingredients: Bacterial diseases are difficult to cure directly — repeated preventive copper application is the main approach
treatment.application_timing: Preventive from just before bloom through shoot growth
treatment.resistance_management: Watch for phytotoxicity from excessive copper use; manage seasonal application totals
treatment.cultural_control: Establish windbreaks, remove infected branches, avoid excess nitrogen fertilization
treatment.phi_note: Copper products are often usable up to 0–1 days before harvest, but this varies by product (confirm via PSIS)
treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.korea_registration_note: Search PSIS (https://psis.rda.go.kr) by 'crop + disease' to confirm the actual registered brand name and exact safety-use standard before use.

treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.korea_registration_note: Search PSIS (https://psis.rda.go.kr) by 'crop + disease' to confirm the actual registered brand name and exact safety-use standard before use.

classes.symptoms: Water-soaked spots on leaves that turn brown and necrotic; raised, scab-like lesions on fruit.
classes.causal_conditions: Hot, humid conditions; spreads via water splash from rain/irrigation and contaminated seed.

treatment.prevention_active_ingredients: Copper-based products; plant-defense inducers (e.g., phosphonate class) can be used alongside
treatment.treatment_active_ingredients: Chemical curative options are limited for bacterial diseases — early prevention is essential
treatment.application_timing: Preventive from early transplanting
treatment.resistance_management: Long-term standalone copper use risks resistant bacterial strains; combine with defense inducers
treatment.cultural_control: Use disease-free seed, rotate crops, switch to drip irrigation to minimize water splash
treatment.phi_note: Copper products are often usable up to 0–3 days before harvest, but this varies by product (confirm via PSIS)
treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.symptoms: Concentric-ring ('target-spot') brown lesions on leaves, progressing upward from lower leaves; severe cases cause early defoliation.
classes.causal_conditions: Hot, humid conditions; higher incidence in nutrient-stressed plants (especially nitrogen deficiency).

treatment.application_timing: Preventive every 7–10 days from mid-season growth
treatment.resistance_management: Avoid repeated strobilurin use; rotate with a protectant
treatment.cultural_control: Crop rotation, balanced fertilization, avoid wetting foliage during irrigation
treatment.phi_note: Typically restricted 7–14 days before harvest (varies by product; confirm via PSIS)
treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.korea_registration_note: Search PSIS (https://psis.rda.go.kr) by 'crop + disease' to confirm the actual registered brand name and exact safety-use standard before use.

classes.symptoms: Water-soaked dark brown lesions on leaves with white fungal growth on the underside; can kill the entire plant rapidly in severe cases. The pathogen behind the Irish Potato Famine.
classes.causal_conditions: Spreads very rapidly in cool (15–20°C), humid weather — intensive management is needed around the late-blight forecast warning period (typically mid-June in Korea).

treatment.prevention_active_ingredients: Protectant fungicides such as mancozeb, chlorothalonil
treatment.treatment_active_ingredients: Curative fungicides such as dimethomorph, ethaboxam — switch immediately if the preventive window is missed and disease appears
treatment.application_method: Foliar spray, applied proactively from the forecast warning period
treatment.application_timing: Begin preventive spraying with protectant fungicides from the late-blight forecast warning period (typically mid-June); switch immediately to a curative product upon confirmed infection
treatment.resistance_management: Rotate protectant and curative classes to suppress resistant strains
treatment.cultural_control: Resistant varieties, immediate removal of infected stems/tubers, improved drainage
treatment.phi_note: Typically restricted 3–14 days before harvest (varies by product; rapid spread requires prompt action; confirm via PSIS)
treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.korea_registration_note: Search PSIS (https://psis.rda.go.kr) by 'crop + disease' to confirm the actual registered brand name and exact safety-use standard before use.

treatment.application_timing: Preventive during high-humidity periods in the greenhouse
treatment.cultural_control: Increase greenhouse ventilation, manage humidity, resistant varieties

classes.symptoms: Small, round, gray-brown spots with light centers and dark margins on leaves; severe defoliation starting from lower leaves.
classes.causal_conditions: Hot, humid conditions; pathogen persists in continuously cropped fields.

treatment.application_timing: Preventive from early transplanting
treatment.cultural_control: Crop rotation, remove infected leaves, mulch to prevent soil splash
treatment.phi_note: Typically restricted 0–7 days before harvest (varies by product; confirm via PSIS)
treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.symptoms: Tiny white-to-yellow stippling on leaves from feeding damage; severe infestations turn leaves bronze with visible webbing.
classes.causal_conditions: Reproduces very rapidly in hot, dry conditions due to a short generation time.

treatment.application_method: Foliar spray, focused on the leaf underside (mites mainly live there)
treatment.application_timing: Begin control immediately at low initial population density
treatment.spray_interval: About 7 days; a second application is recommended to control newly hatched mites
treatment.resistance_management: Resistance develops very quickly with repeated use of the same mode of action — always rotate between different classes
treatment.cultural_control: Use natural predators (e.g., predatory mites), maintain humidity through irrigation (mites favor dry conditions)
treatment.phi_note: Typically restricted 1–7 days before harvest (varies by product; confirm via PSIS)
treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.korea_registration_note: Search PSIS (https://psis.rda.go.kr) by 'crop + disease' to confirm the actual registered brand name and exact safety-use standard before use.

classes.symptoms: Concentric-ring ('target-spot') brown lesions on leaves; fruit can also develop sunken lesions.
classes.causal_conditions: Hot, humid conditions; higher incidence in densely planted fields.

treatment.application_timing: Preventive during mid-season growth
treatment.cultural_control: Improve airflow, remove infected leaves, crop rotation
treatment.phi_note: Typically restricted 0–7 days before harvest (varies by product; confirm via PSIS)
treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.korea_registration_note: Search PSIS (https://psis.rda.go.kr) by 'crop + disease' to confirm the actual registered brand name and exact safety-use standard before use.

classes.symptoms: Upward-curling, yellowing leaves; stunted plant growth; poor fruit set.
classes.causal_conditions: Spreads when whitefly vector populations are high. No direct cure exists — vector control is essential.

treatment.prevention_active_ingredients: No direct treatment exists — insecticide-based vector (whitefly) control is the key management tool
treatment.treatment_active_ingredients: Neonicotinoid-class and other insecticides targeting the whitefly vector
treatment.application_method: Foliar spray guided by vector scouting, combined with exclusion netting
treatment.application_timing: Preventing whitefly entry at early transplanting is critical; treat immediately if detected
treatment.spray_interval: Adjust based on observed vector density
treatment.cultural_control: Remove infected plants early, install exclusion netting, resistant varieties, vector control
treatment.phi_note: Safety-use intervals for vector-control insecticides vary by active ingredient (confirm via PSIS)
treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.korea_registration_note: Search PSIS (https://psis.rda.go.kr) by 'crop + disease' to confirm the actual registered brand name and exact safety-use standard before use.

classes.pathogen: ToMV (virus, spread by contact and contaminated tools)
classes.symptoms: Mottled light-and-dark green mosaic pattern on leaves; leaf deformity; reduced growth.
classes.causal_conditions: Commonly spread via contact through hands and tools. No direct cure exists.

treatment.prevention_active_ingredients: No chemical control exists — prevention is essential
treatment.application_timing: not applicable
treatment.cultural_control: Remove infected plants immediately, disinfect tools between uses, use certified disease-free seed/transplants, wash hands after tobacco use (to prevent cross-contamination from tobacco-related viruses)
treatment.phi_note: Not applicable (no chemical control exists)
treatment.ppe_note: Wear a mask, safety goggles, rubber gloves, and protective clothing during application. Wash hands and face with soap afterward. Avoid spraying during peak midday heat and spray with the wind at your back. No single operator should spray continuously for more than 2 hours.

classes.korea_registration_note: Search PSIS (https://psis.rda.go.kr) by 'crop + disease' to confirm the actual registered brand name and exact safety-use standard before use.

classes.symptoms: No notable symptoms

treatment.cultural_control: Regular scouting recommended
